summary: Bootstrapped file-backed collaboration workspace for Codex, Claude Code, and future LangGraph orchestration.
Started local Agent Deck launcher task for opening Codex and Claude Code together in one terminal.
Installed tmux and Agent Deck user launcher; verified script syntax, tmux availability, installed paths, and desktop entry validity.
Updated Agent Deck to include Gemini as a third tmux pane alongside Codex and Claude Code.
Implemented a minimal executable LangGraph runner around the file-backed task graph and added the langgraph-run command-center helper.
Allowed the command center to use named temporary helper agents while keeping Codex, Claude, and Gemini as the core agents.
Started LangGraph command-center routing upgrade for runtime policy, helper proposals, and explicit event recording.
Implemented LangGraph routing upgrade with runtime policy loading, helper note scanning, helper proposal route, recommended command hints, and explicit event recording.
Routed workflow to propose_helper: Consider opening a temporary helper for TASK-001; keep a core agent accountable for integration.
Added durable agent availability state and unavailable-agent routing so work can continue around session-limited agents unless a task explicitly requires them.
Updated AI Command Center launches so core agents and helpers open in visible terminals by default, added ai screen and ai approve commands for approval prompts, and installed the updated ai wrapper.
Added an AI Help option to the command-center banner and grouped ai help output, with commands and ai-help aliases.
Added optional Antigravity support to the AI Command Center, including ai antigravity start, messaging, helper launch support, help text, and a clear missing-CLI warning.
Replaced Gemini with Antigravity as the third command-center core agent, added antigravity and agy PATH wrappers, updated WezTerm and Agent Deck launchers, and installed the updated scripts.
Fixed Antigravity command-center launch mode: it now runs as a foreground GUI tab with Electron logging enabled instead of hcom agy, which exited before binding.
Removed Gemini from current command-center plans and durable core-agent availability state because it cannot communicate reliably through hcom.
Restored Gemini to command-center planning state as manually coordinated, with Antigravity and Gemini both requiring operator prompting when hcom communication is not reliable.
Marked agent codex standby.
Marked agent antigravity standby.
Completed bootstrap review. Bootstrap APPROVED with no blockers. TASK-002 and TASK-003 unblocked.

Designed SQLite task board migration: full schema with 14 tables, atomic claim protocol, lease expiration, 4-phase migration path from file-backed to SQLite, seed script outline, and triage of 4 open questions.
All 12 bootstrap tasks done. Proposed TASK-013 (SQLite seed script) as the Phase 1 implementation step from the SQLite migration design.
Marked agent codex available.
Gemini and Antigravity marked long-term unavailable per operator. Codex and Claude are the two active agents for the project. TASK-013 assigned to Codex.
Implemented SQLite seed script and schema, created MAP_System/map.db, verified all designed tables, seeded current file-backed state, and confirmed repeat runs do not duplicate rows.
TASK-013 APPROVED. Schema, seed script, and idempotency all verified. No blockers.
Proposed TASK-014 (SQLite claim module), TASK-015 (runner reads SQLite), TASK-016 (validator fix + claiming docs). Codex owns 014+015; Claude owns 016.
Implemented SQLite atomic claim helpers with claim, heartbeat, submit, release, and lease-expiration behavior, verified against a temporary schema-backed database.
TASK-014 APPROVED. All 7 claim edge cases verified live. TASK-015 and TASK-016 now unblocked.
Routed workflow to wait_or_reconcile: No dependency-satisfied ready work found; reconcile stale claims or wait.
Updated LangGraph runner to load tasks and agents from SQLite when map.db exists, fall back to JSON when absent, and write --record-event route events to SQLite as well as events.jsonl.
Fixed validate_task_graph.py stall/all-done false positive and pre-existing KeyError on unknown dependencies. Updated AGENTS.md with SQLite claiming protocol. Added DEC-009 to decisions.md.
Routed workflow to review: Send submitted tasks to an independent reviewer.
TASK-015 APPROVED. SQLite read path, JSON fallback, and dual --record-event write all verified live. Two optional findings only. TASK-016 also done (validator fix + AGENTS.md + DEC-009).
Proposed TASK-017 through TASK-020. Codex: reconcile script (017) + SQLite export (018). Claude: software template (019) + autonomous claim loop design (020). All READY, no dependencies.
Designed software project workflow template: 5-stage sequence (spec→implement→test→review→release), task type definitions, approval gates, review standard, worked example.
Designed autonomous claim loop protocol: full poll-claim-heartbeat-submit cycle, 6 failure modes, configuration schema, pseudocode, open questions for TASK-021 implementer.
Expired stale task leases: count=0; task_ids=none.
Implemented one-shot SQLite lease reconciliation script with JSONL and SQLite event logging; verified no-op and expired-lease paths.
Implemented SQLite-to-file export script for tasks, task graph, and agent status; verified dry-run, first export, and repeat idempotency.
TASK-019 APPROVED. Software workflow template parses as YAML and covers stage sequence, task types, gates, review standard, and worked example.
TASK-020 APPROVED. Autonomous claim-loop protocol covers poll-claim-heartbeat-submit cycle, failure modes, reconcile interaction, shutdown, and implementation outputs.
Implemented LangGraph-driven autonomous claim loop with MemorySaver, SQLite claiming, heartbeat, submit/export, claim-race handling, handler-failure release, and shutdown release.
TASK-022 reject resume path does not persist rejection: --reject reports rejected but approval_gates.status remains pending. Required fix before approval.
Expired stale task leases: count=0; task_ids=none.
Expired stale task leases: count=1; task_ids=TEST-EXPIRE.
APPROVED. Reconcile script verified: no-op and expired-lease fixture both passed, dual logging confirmed.
APPROVED. Export idempotency confirmed (22 unchanged on repeat run). System agent exclusion correct.
APPROVED by codex. Software project workflow template.
APPROVED by codex. Autonomous claim loop design doc.
Created TASK-021 (cyclic LangGraph claim loop, Codex) and TASK-022 (interrupt+SqliteSaver runner upgrade, Claude). Both IN_PROGRESS.
TASK-022 submitted. MapSqliteSaver + interrupt()/Command in runner. All 5 tests pass including no-regression, checkpointed run, interrupt+approve+gate-status-update.
TASK-022 resubmitted after R-01 fix: check_approval_gates now records rejection (decision is not None guard)
TASK-022 APPROVED after re-review. Reject resume now persists approval_gates.status='rejected'; py_compile and checkpointed reject resume verified.
TASK-022 approved by codex-zumi. MapSqliteSaver + interrupt()/Command + --reject SQLite persistence all verified.
TASK-021 resubmitted after review fixes: claim_failed/no_ready_task route back to reconcile in daemon mode, operator interrupts are surfaced, and unreachable dry-run submit code was removed.
TASK-021 approved. All three review findings resolved: R-01 daemon routing fix, R-02 interrupt surface, R-03 dead code removed.

Implemented agent_loop.py PID lock guard keyed by agent_id and resolved DB path. Verified live-lock rejection, stale-lock cleanup, and clean-exit release. Full validator currently blocked by unrelated TASK-026/TASK-027 decisions.md output collision.
TASK-025 rename incomplete: installed langgraph-run wrapper and MAP_System/graph/README.md still point to deleted MAP_System/langgraph/runner.py path.
TASK-027 shared-doc cleanup missed requirements.md; it still says no fully autonomous daemon yet despite TASK-021 and AGENTS.md documenting the daemon.
TASK-025 APPROVED after re-review. langgraph-run wrapper and graph README now use MAP_System/graph/runner.py; langgraph-run --pretty and py_compile pass.
TASK-027 APPROVED after re-review. requirements.md stale autonomous-daemon non-goal fixed, output paths updated, and task graph validation passes.

Implemented STATE_SNAPSHOT YAML schema/template with Claude handoff example, filled DEC-010, and added AGENTS emit/load instructions. YAML validation passes; full graph validation currently blocked by unrelated TASK-023/TASK-024 agent_loop.py output collision.

TASK-023 integration test passes but leaves workflow/task_graph.json contaminated with TASK-IT-01, causing validate_task_graph failure after the test. Restore all mutated workspace files or isolate export output.
TASK-024 approved by Claude. PID lock guard verified: O_CREAT|O_EXCL live lock rejection, stale PID cleanup, finally release, and LockHeld exit 2.
TASK-023 APPROVED after re-review. Integration test uses export_to_files --output-dir for temp isolation; 11/11 pass and validate_task_graph remains clean after test.

Implemented map-status CLI with live IN_PROGRESS claims, SUBMITTED reviews, pending gates, and agent statuses. py_compile, real map.db run, and temp fixture pass; full graph validation currently blocked by TASK-028/TASK-032 agent_loop.py output collision.
Implemented run_tests.sh CI runner: py_compile all MAP_System Python files, validate_task_graph, integration_test, per-check pass/fail, final summary. Current workspace exits 0 with pass=3 fail=0.
TASK-028 --resume fails against an interrupted checkpoint with LangGraph UnboundLocalError resume_is_map. Required before approval.
Implemented map-task CLI for task create/show/approve/reject, event emission, and file sync. Temp DB fixture covers success and bad-state failure; py_compile and validate_task_graph pass.
TASK-025 submitted. Renamed MAP_System/langgraph/ to MAP_System/graph/. Updated agent_loop.py RUNNER path. py_compile clean, runner+agent_loop smoke tests pass.

TASK-027 submitted. Updated unresolved_questions (2 resolved), decisions (DEC-004 note, DEC-010 reserved), AGENTS.md (wezterm-tab policy, autonomous daemon section, MATOCP communication table, runner path).
TASK-023 submitted. 11/11 integration tests pass. Added --db to runner.py CLI + agent_loop run_runner() passes it. Temp DB isolated: no manual setup needed.
TASK-026 approved. STATE_SNAPSHOT schema + worked example verified; YAML parses; all required keys present. DEC-010 filled, AGENTS.md updated.
Marked agent antigravity available.
Marked antigravity available after operator requested reintroduction.
Assigned antigravity navo review scope for TASK-031 and TASK-033.
Marked antigravity standby because it is out of tokens again; routing should not depend on it.
Requested Claude review for submitted TASK-031 and TASK-033; Antigravity unavailable, so routing continues with Codex+Claude only.

TASK-028 resubmitted. Fix: Command(resume=True) instead of Command(resume=None) to avoid LangGraph UnboundLocalError on resume_is_map. Verified: first run interrupts correctly (RC=0), --resume exits 0. run_tests.sh pass=3 fail=0, integration 11/11.
TASK-028 approved by codex.
Unblocked TASK-032 after dependency TASK-028 was approved; preparing Codex implementation.
Added TASK-032 retry cooldown test artifact.

TASK-032 submitted. Added retry cooldown for handler CalledProcessError using LoopState retry_cooldowns, --retry-cooldown default 300 and 0 disable. Verified targeted cooldown/disable fixtures and run_tests.sh pass=3 fail=0.

TASK-030 submitted. Multi-gate: check_approval_gates loops with one interrupt() per gate; each payload lists all remaining. --approve/--reject take GATE_ID, --thread-id always explicit. Loop-back implicit via re-interrupt. Single-gate unchanged. run_tests.sh pass=3 fail=0, 4/4 targeted tests pass.
TASK-031 approved by claude-bono.
TASK-032 approved by claude-bono.
TASK-033 approved by claude-bono.
TASK-030 rejected by codex: Multi-gate reject path records the rejected gate as approved in SQLite; see MAP_System/artifacts/reviews/task030_review.md.
TASK-030 approved by claude-bono.

TASK-030 approved after fix. Root cause: pending_gates reloaded from SQLite on re-execution shrinks the interrupt count, so interrupt positions shift. Fix: load_all_approval_gates iterates all gates in stable rowid order; resolved gates return stored values immediately, maintaining consistent interrupt positions. GATE-A=approved, GATE-B=rejected fixture passes. run_tests.sh pass=3 fail=0.
Created TASK-034: Add multi-gate approval regression test

Submitted TASK-034. Added multigate_regression_test.py and wired run_tests.sh. Verification: run_tests.sh passes 4/4. ## Improvements Checked Implemented: targeted approve-then-reject regression for multi-gate approval ordering. Recommended: independent review by Claude. Not changed: runner behavior beyond test coverage.
TASK-034 approved by claude-muse.

Submitted TASK-035. Implemented HPOM READY promotion gate, audit mode, claim-time acceptance-criteria soft gate, map_task incomplete-create NEEDS_SHAPING default, and deterministic tests wired into run_tests.sh. Full run_tests.sh passes pass=5 fail=0.

Submitted TASK-036. Added read-only local assistant health check and ai local-health command. Health reports Ollama reachable, all five required models available, Aider available, and local assistants remain helper-capability-only. Full run_tests.sh passes pass=5 fail=0.

Applied AI Command Center Lab feedback: documented visible helper launch rule, added lab README/AGENTS, updated lab agent prompts, and made ai helper launches reject HCOM_TERMINAL=headless unless AI_ALLOW_HEADLESS=1 is explicitly set.
Improved AI Command Center Lab with ai-command-center-lab-health, exposed it in the Control Lab shell and docs, and verified 23 health checks passed with 0 warnings and 0 failures.

Added ai-command-center-lab-status for on-demand operator snapshots: health summary, live hcom agents, recent lab messages, and recent MAP session events. Verified status command, health command, and shell syntax; health remains 23 passed, 0 warnings, 0 failures.

Captured operator feedback that MAP needs a Git operation coordination lock so multiple agents do not attempt overlapping commit/push work on the same repository.

Refined operator feedback: MAP intake must route by worker fit across Codex, Claude, local models, helpers, or human operator before assigning ownership; updated backlog wording accordingly.

Added map_task.py log TASK-NNN subcommand: assembles task record + event timeline + linked artifacts (reviews, releases, handoffs) into one readable view. Submitted for nora review. Note: work was done before proper MAP claim — protocol failure acknowledged.

Started DarkMellow project, backed up live /home/home/.themes/Mellow-Dark as Projects/DarkMellow/backups/Dark Mellow Testing, compared feature coverage against Mint-Y-Dark and Mint-L-Dark-Grey, and recorded recommendations for operator decision. No live theme files changed.

Left self STATE_SNAPSHOT for future resume. Snapshot records full Mint parity live apply, verification, key artifacts, known limitations, and that claude-lab-kona now owns remaining cosmetic DarkMellow items to avoid duplicate work.

Localhost-only default, no shell injection, solid input validation on /api/hcom/send, textContent-only rendering (no XSS), path traversal guarded. One issue requested before done: hcom sender identity is hardcoded to 'maki' in hcom_list()/send_hcom(), so anyone using the app - including the operator - sends/lists as codex-lab-maki, breaking MAP audit attribution now that a human will drive this UI directly. Recommended making identity a startup parameter. Two minor non-blocking hardening notes (path traversal check robustness, no-auth/0.0.0.0 caveat) also filed. Full writeup: Projects/CommandCenterUI/artifacts/task056-review-taro.md.

Re-reviewed after fix: HCOM_AGENT_NAME now defaults to 'browser' and hcom_list()/send_hcom() explicitly refuse (fail-safe, clear error) unless a real identity is set via --agent-name or COMMAND_CENTER_UI_AGENT_NAME env var; run-command-center-app.sh passes it through with the same safe default. translate_path also switched to Path.relative_to() instead of string-prefix matching, addressing the minor traversal-check note. Approved - both requested/noted issues resolved. No-auth/0.0.0.0 caveat remains a documented, non-blocking note.

Created TASK-058: Fix Dark_Mellow wallpaper install settings (READY)

Reviewed codex-lab-maki's install-mellow-dark.sh wallpaper fix (Dark_Mellow repo). Matches the root cause diagnosed for the operator's cross-PC report (picture-options not set, so a solid color was shown instead of the wallpaper). Patch gates each gsettings set behind 'gsettings writable <schema> <key>' checks before writing, covering org.cinnamon.desktop.background and org.gnome.desktop.background picture-uri/picture-options, plus org.gnome...picture-uri-dark - skips cleanly on systems missing a schema instead of erroring. bash -n clean, single small diff, no side effects beyond desktop wallpaper settings. Approved. Non-blocking follow-up idea: org.cinnamon.desktop.background may have its own picture-uri-dark analog on newer Cinnamon that this patch doesn't set - worth adding only if the operator's system still shows the issue after this fix.
Created TASK-059: Review Dark_Mellow package and capture MAP insights (READY)

Deep follow-up review at operator request, beyond the original TASK-056/057 passes. Found a real gap I missed earlier: no Origin/CSRF protection on POST /api/hcom/send in app/server.py - any other page open in the same browser could silently POST an hcom send once the app runs in send-enabled (real identity) mode, without the operator's awareness (classic local-server drive-by CSRF). Also found unhandled ValueError on malformed 'limit' query param / Content-Length header (do_GET/do_POST) causing raw failures instead of clean 400s, and a minor asymmetry in install-mellow-dark.sh (fonts overwritten without the backup-before-write pattern used for theme/pictures). Full writeup: Projects/CommandCenterUI/artifacts/task056-deep-review-taro.md.
Created TASK-060: Patch CommandCenterUI hcom send CSRF guard (READY)
Patched CommandCenterUI /api/hcom/send CSRF guard and numeric input validation; validation artifact recorded.
Corrected review task owner from implementer to requester to satisfy MAP self-review claim gate.

Reviewed codex-lab-maki's CSRF fix for CommandCenterUI (app/server.py). same_origin_request() checks Origin first (against a set built from Host header + actual bound host:port + 127.0.0.1/localhost aliases), falls back to Referer with a fail-closed check if malformed. Correctly blocks the threat model flagged (a cross-site page's form/no-cors POST to localhost) since modern browsers always attach Origin on cross-origin POST regardless of referrer-policy. bounded_int()/try-except wrapping fixes the malformed limit/Content-Length crash cleanly. Approved. Non-blocking hardening note: when BOTH Origin and Referer are absent, same_origin_request() currently returns True (allows the request) - this doesn't weaken the CSRF protection (browsers always send Origin on POST) but does mean a local script/process curling the endpoint directly bypasses the origin check entirely, on top of the already-documented no-auth/loopback-only caveat. Flipping that branch to return False costs nothing (the app's own fetch calls always send Origin) and closes the gap fully - suggested as a quick follow-up, not a blocker.

Limit watcher implemented and running (pid in .locks/limit-watcher.pid, 60s interval): polls agents/status.json for out_of_tokens+resume_after, auto-resumes agents via hcom r --terminal wezterm-tab --go (one nudge per window, never headless), reports unparseable reset times and silent hcom stops as BLOCKED events. 7 new tests, suite 19/19, task graph passes. TASK-080 resubmitted after CHANGES_REQUESTED. Finding 1 fixed: hcom_live_agents() now returns [] on successful-empty (silent-stop detection runs when ALL agents vanish) vs None only on genuine hcom failure; regression test added (8 watcher tests, suite 19/19). Finding 2 root-caused and hardened: the original watcher (275543) never actually died -- reviewer's sandbox runs in a PID namespace where outside PIDs look dead and its restart wrote namespace-PID 9 into the pidfile. Launcher now identity-verifies via /proc/<pid>/cmdline (immune to PID reuse AND namespace false-negatives), detects/removes stale pidfiles, self-verifies after start, and runs python3 -u so the log shows liveness. Old watcher killed, fixed watcher restarted (280466) and verified: identity-checked PID + state-file mtime advancing exactly 60s across a poll cycle.

Created TASK-083: RnS v2: detect silent limit-stops and probe-resume without a recorded reset time (READY)

RnS v2 implemented after the overnight incident (operator #15260): root cause was that the session died with no final turn (nothing written to status.json) while hcom kept listing it, so neither v1 path fired -- watcher state confirms last_live never changed all night. v2 classifies liveness from hcom list --json status + status_age_seconds (stale >30min = down even while listed), opens incidents for presumed-down agents, tail-reads the session transcript for the limit message's reset time when available, and otherwise probe-resumes on a capped 15/45/90/150/240/330-minute backoff before giving up loudly. 4 new tests including one reproducing the exact overnight shape; 12 watcher tests, suite 22/22. Watcher restarted on v2 (pid 312827). Protocol note branded Rise & Shine per operator.
